name: Dulce Papers
description: The Dulce Papers refer to a collection of purported documents and testimonies alleging the existence of a secret underground base near Dulce, New Mexico, that houses joint human-alien research facilities. The core claim is that this base is the site of unethical experiments on humans and animals, as well as collaboration between the U.S. government and various extraterrestrial species, often described as Reptilians and Greys. The theory gained prominence in the late 1980s and early 1990s, largely through UFOlogy circles and publications. Proponents cite supposed leaked documents (the 'Dulce Papers' themselves), testimonies from individuals claiming to be former employees of the base (such as Phil Schneider and Thomas Castello), and alleged anomalous activity reported in the Dulce area as evidence. These accounts describe genetic engineering, mind control experiments, and interactions with hostile alien entities. Mainstream perspectives dismiss the Dulce Papers as a hoax or elaborate urban legend. Skeptics point to the lack of verifiable physical evidence, inconsistencies in the testimonies, and the absence of any credible documentation supporting the existence of such a facility. The alleged documents have been widely scrutinized and deemed forgeries or fabrications. The entire narrative is generally considered to be a product of misinformation, misinterpretation, and the human desire to find explanations for unexplained phenomena. The Dulce Papers continue to circulate within conspiracy theory communities, serving as a focal point for beliefs about government secrecy, alien contact, and clandestine technological advancements.
subtitle: Alleged alien-human base conspiracy theory

claims.claim: A secret underground base exists in Dulce, New Mexico.
claims.evidence: Supposed leaked documents, testimonies of purported former employees.
claims.criticism: No verifiable physical evidence, inconsistent testimonies, documents deemed forgeries.

claims.claim: The base houses joint human-alien research facilities.
claims.evidence: Accounts of genetic engineering, mind control experiments, and alien interactions.
claims.criticism: Lack of credible evidence, reliance on unverifiable claims and anecdotal evidence.

claims.claim: Unethical experiments are conducted on humans and animals at the base.
claims.evidence: Testimonies describing gruesome experiments and alien involvement.
claims.criticism: Absence of corroborating evidence, implausibility of large-scale secret operations without detection.

keyFigures.name: Phil Schneider
keyFigures.role: Claimed to be a former government geologist who worked at Dulce.

keyFigures.name: Thomas Castello
keyFigures.role: Alleged former security officer at the Dulce base who provided detailed accounts.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who investigated some aspects of the Dulce story.

keyFigures.name: Jerome Clark
keyFigures.role: Ufologist and skeptic who critically examined the Dulce claims.

timeline.event: The Dulce Papers and related claims begin to circulate within UFOlogy circles.
The Dulce story gains wider attention through publications and conferences.
The Dulce conspiracy theory continues to be discussed and debated online.

sources.author: Greg Bishop
sources.work: Project Beta: The Story of Paul Bennewitz, National Security, and Alien Abductions
sources.title: Greg Bishop
